dropped.reason: too short to carry a claim

meta.model: Gemini gemini-flash-lite-latest · US (and trains on free-tier prompts)

clusters.label: iPad typing and cursor jumping bugs
Mobile app lag, freezing, and crashing
Forced AI features and obtrusive search bar
Mandatory account creation and login walls

insights.title: Aggressive AI promotion and unremovable bottom toolbars ruin clean workspace layouts.
insights.summary: Users are deeply frustrated by the mandatory placement of generative AI search bars and chat prompts across the primary interface real estate. Long-time enthusiasts note that these intrusive UI changes disrupt clean navigation and frequently result in accidental taps. Because there is no simple settings toggle to hide these elements, users feel micromanaged and alienated by forced monetization tactics. Many are actively searching for alternative note-taking applications that prioritize human editing over automated features.

evidence.name: HU
evidence.quote: The button for AI is large and centered on the bottom of your screen instead of a search bar like it used to be. There was also a way to turn it off prior and now there isn't.…

evidence.name: PT
evidence.quote: AI features are garbage. The search being AI first and actual page search in a smaller icon that requires two clicks is the worst UI design i’ve seen in any app ever. Horrific…

evidence.name: AS
evidence.quote: used to love notion! it was my favorite place to write and plan, but the focus on ai completely ruined the layout that made this app so awesome. i don’t mind the idea of ai being…

recommendation.cost: These are paying subscribers and loyalists of years who feel disrespected by forced software changes, stating they are migrating their entire second brains to competing markdown apps.
recommendation.action: Add a dedicated settings toggle that hides the bottom AI prompt bar and restores the traditional navigation layout.
recommendation.rationale: Users are not asking to remove AI completely from the platform, but rather requesting control over screen real estate so they can focus on their notes without interference.
recommendation.signal: Support tickets and low-star ratings regarding obtrusive AI placement and missing home buttons cease.

insights.title: Severe loading lag and constant application crashes break core productivity workflows.
insights.summary: Mobile users across platforms experience frustrating slowdowns, unresponsive interface elements, and automatic page refreshes that disrupt writing sessions. Large documents and database views trigger infinite loading states or force users to repeatedly restart the application just to view basic content. This lack of responsiveness transforms a dependable productivity tool into an unreliable burden. Consequently, users lose confidence in mobile synchronization and data availability.

evidence.name: R
evidence.quote: The worst part about this app is that just when you start depending on it for something important it starts glitching in some new and horribly frustrating way. Don’t waste your…

evidence.name: MQ
evidence.quote: Consistently has been one of the most laggy and buggy apps on my phone. Lots of features, but the core functionality of just editing a file takes so long as nothing seems to be…

evidence.name: DA
evidence.quote: It is almost impossible to use Notion on iPad. Page icons are not loading, sometimes it lagging so badly I can’t even type simple text on pages. Often shows that I’m offline, but…

recommendation.cost: These are busy professionals on-site who depend on instant access, losing trust in mobile reliability and cancelling paid subscriptions when their data fails to load.
recommendation.action: Optimize local caching and database rendering pipelines to eliminate freeze states and unprompted page refreshes on mobile.
recommendation.rationale: The reviews consistently highlight that crashes happen during ordinary text editing and scrolling, indicating memory leaks in the client-side database views rather than server-side network errors.
recommendation.signal: Crash reports drop and reviews stop describing frozen screens or forced app restarts during active editing sessions.

insights.title: Recent updates render the iPad app virtually unusable due to erratic text input bugs.
insights.summary: iPad users relying on hardware or software keyboards find themselves unable to write because typing triggers an immediate screen jump away from the cursor. This disruptive layout shifting hides the text input area entirely, making basic note-taking impossible. Because users cannot see what they are typing, many are abandoning the mobile app in favor of competitors or desktop alternatives. This regression severely harms the utility of tablet use for students and professionals.

evidence.name: YU
evidence.quote: Whenever I type, the screen goes down. I hope this can be fixed.

evidence.name: KR
evidence.quote: Update 21/06/26. Had to lower the rating because for the last month or so Notion stopped working properly with my iPad when I use a Bluetooth keyboard - the pages no longer allow…

evidence.name: XE
evidence.quote: Eigentlich liebe ich Notion und nutze es sowohl über Web, iPhone und iPad. Leider scheint es seit einiger Zeit viele Probleme mit der iPad Version zu geben. Bei Texteingabe friert…

recommendation.cost: These are daily power users and students walking away from the mobile interface entirely, abandoning their workflow mid-semester because they can no longer take notes on their tablets.
recommendation.action: Fix the view-port auto-scroll trigger that fires on keyboard focus events within iPadOS text editing views.
recommendation.rationale: Reviewers explicitly link the sudden screen drop to typing interactions rather than general rendering lag, pointing to a faulty focus management change in recent codebases.
recommendation.signal: One-star reviews mentioning screen jumping and hidden text while typing on iPad disappear entirely.
